label: Jurisdictions
description: Canonical jurisdiction model for scoping source validity, operational legality and localization review.
usageNotes: Use the most specific jurisdiction available.
If a source is multinational, include multiple jurisdiction codes rather than forcing `global` unless truly transnational.

entries.label: Global
entries.description: Multi-jurisdiction or broadly international source not bound to a single territory.
usageNotes: Use when the source intentionally spans jurisdictions.

entries.label: United States
usageNotes: Use for US-guideline and reimbursement-aligned sources.

entries.label: Mexico
usageNotes: Use for Mexican regulatory and clinical governance alignment.

entries.label: European Union
usageNotes: Use when guidance is framed at EU level.

entries.label: United Kingdom
usageNotes: Use for NICE and UK-focused care models.

entries.label: Canada
usageNotes: Use for Canadian guideline and HTA contexts.

entries.label: Latin America
usageNotes: Use only when the source truly spans multiple LATAM countries.

entries.label: Institutional Local
entries.description: Local hospital or network governance boundary.
usageNotes: Highest implementation specificity; requires local governance provenance.
